sources.observed: Node.js exposes exclusive creation, file synchronization, rename, and directory handles needed for a durable replacement protocol.
Crash-safe commit protocols separate temporary state from the committed image and require an explicit commit boundary.
Trusted root rotation requires sequential authorization; persistence must not allow two competing N+1 roots to become trusted.

finding: Authorization and persistence are separate trust boundaries. A valid N+1 root must be committed with compare-and-swap against the exact trusted-root digest, under a single-writer lock, using a synchronized temporary file followed by rename and directory synchronization.
design_implication: Store the canonical root bytes with a SHA-256 digest and previous-digest link. Reject stale writers, ignore incomplete temporary files during recovery, and fail closed on digest mismatch.

cases: only one concurrent N+1 writer commits

uncertainty: Filesystem rename and directory-sync behavior remains platform and filesystem dependent; network filesystems and Windows semantics require separate qualification.
falsification_route: Run the same test suite under concurrent child processes and inject termination after temp-file fsync, after rename, and before directory fsync on each target filesystem. Any state where both candidates become trusted, a partial record becomes trusted, or the previous valid root disappears falsifies the design.
